pattern: With only one negative balance ever present, every unit of relief it needs can only travel toward it from one of two directions around the circle, so the cheapest way to cover the deficit is to pull units from whichever neighbors are closest first, going farther only once the closer supply runs out.
hints: If the total of every balance is negative there simply isn't enough to go around, so no sequence of moves can fix it.

When nothing is negative, no moves are needed at all; otherwise there is exactly one deficit to fill, and every other position's units can reach it going either clockwise or counter-clockwise around the circle -- moving a unit costs exactly the number of steps of whichever direction is shorter.

Sort the other positions by that shorter distance and take from the closest ones first, only reaching further away once the nearer supply is exhausted -- this greedy order minimizes the total number of moves.

constraints.note: at most one entry is negative in the given array

edge_inputs.description: statement example three, impossible because the total is negative
single person, already non-negative, nothing to do
single person, negative with no one else to help
no negative balance at all, answer is zero even though nothing is uniform
total exactly zero, the deficit is exactly coverable with no surplus left over
the deficit sits between two equally-close neighbors on a small ring, so ties in distance must be handled correctly
values at the maximum allowed magnitude

stress.description: random rings with one negative entry placed at a random position
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    balance = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    neg_i = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    balance[neg_i] = -rng.randint(1, 1000 * n if n > 0 else 1)
    return [balance]

stress.description: random rings that are deliberately infeasible (total negative)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 3000))
    balance = [rng.randint(0, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    neg_i = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    balance[neg_i] = -(sum(balance) + rng.randint(1, 1000))
    return [balance]
